name: postgres mcp pro tool-selection matrix
description: Compare stock and tuned tool descriptions for Postgres MCP Pro schema, SQL execution, explain, workload, index tuning, and health workflows.

instruction_variants.instructions: Choose the single Postgres MCP Pro tool that should be called next. Prefer schema/introspection tools before writing exploratory SQL when the user lacks table details. Use execute_sql only when the immediate next action is to run SQL. Use explain_query for execution plans, analyze_query_indexes for index recommendations on supplied SQL, analyze_workload_indexes for index recommendations from observed workload, and analyze_db_health for whole-database health checks.
instruction_variants.name: postgres_host_rules

tools.name: list_schemas
tools.purpose: Lists all database schemas available in the PostgreSQL instance.

tools.name: list_objects
tools.purpose: Lists database objects (tables, views, sequences, extensions) within a specified schema.

object_type.description: Database object type such as table, view, sequence, or extension

tools.name: get_object_details
tools.purpose: Provides information about a specific database object, for example, a table's columns, constraints, and indexes.

tools.name: execute_sql
tools.purpose: Executes SQL statements on the database, with read-only limitations when connected in restricted mode.

hypothetical_indexes.description: Optional hypothetical indexes to include while planning

tools.name: explain_query
tools.purpose: Gets the execution plan for a SQL query describing how PostgreSQL will process it and exposing the query planner's cost model. Can be invoked with hypothetical indexes to simulate the behavior after adding indexes.

order_by.description: Metric to rank queries, such as total_time or mean_time

limit.description: Maximum number of queries to return

tools.name: get_top_queries
tools.purpose: Reports the slowest SQL queries based on total execution time using pg_stat_statements data.

max_index_count.description: Optional maximum number of indexes to recommend

tools.name: analyze_workload_indexes
tools.purpose: Analyzes the database workload to identify resource-intensive queries, then recommends optimal indexes for them.

queries.description: Specific SQL queries to analyze, up to 10

tools.name: analyze_query_indexes
tools.purpose: Analyzes a list of specific SQL queries (up to 10) and recommends optimal indexes for them.

tools.name: analyze_db_health
tools.purpose: Performs comprehensive health checks including: buffer cache hit rates, connection health, constraint validation, index health (duplicate/unused/invalid), sequence limits, and vacuum health.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when the schema is already known or the user asks about objects inside a named schema.
tools.name: list_schemas
tools.purpose: Discover which schemas exist in the connected Postgres database.
quality_checks: Use before list_objects when the user does not name a schema.
Do not use to inspect tables, columns, indexes, or run SQL.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks what schemas are available or when a schema must be chosen before deeper inspection.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for column/index/constraint details on one object; use get_object_details. Avoid for running SQL.

schema_name.description: Exact schema to list, for example public or analytics.

tools.name: list_objects
tools.purpose: List tables, views, sequences, and extensions inside one schema.
quality_checks: Use for object inventory, not object internals.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks what database objects are in a named schema.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for a broad schema inventory, SQL execution, explain plans, or workload tuning.

object_name.description: Exact object name, for example orders.

object_type.description: Object type such as table, view, sequence, or extension.

tools.name: get_object_details
tools.purpose: Inspect one database object's columns, constraints, indexes, and related metadata.
quality_checks: Use for table structure before generating joins or filters.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks for details about a specific table, view, sequence, or extension.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for schema discovery, execution plans without running, index recommendations, top-query reports, or database health checks.

sql.description: The SQL statement to run now.

tools.name: execute_sql
tools.purpose: Run a concrete SQL statement against the connected database.
quality_checks: Only use when SQL execution is the next step.
In restricted/production contexts, prefer read-only SELECT statements.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks to run, query, fetch, count, or update data and the needed SQL is ready.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when the user asks to actually run the query, find top workload queries, or produce index recommendations.

hypothetical_indexes.description: Optional hypothetical indexes to simulate in the plan.

sql.description: SQL query to explain without executing for results.

tools.name: explain_query
tools.purpose: Get PostgreSQL's execution plan and planner cost for a specific SQL query.
quality_checks: If the user wants indexes recommended, use analyze_query_indexes instead.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks how Postgres will execute a supplied query or wants to compare plans with hypothetical indexes.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when the user already supplied specific SQL to tune; use analyze_query_indexes. Avoid for health checks.

order_by.description: Ranking metric such as total_time or mean_time.

limit.description: Maximum number of queries to return.

tools.name: get_top_queries
tools.purpose: Rank observed SQL statements from pg_stat_statements by slow or expensive execution metrics.
quality_checks: Use to identify workload candidates before tuning.
Do not imply index recommendations are produced by this call.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks which queries are slowest, most expensive, or consuming the most total time.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when the user supplied the exact SQL to tune; use analyze_query_indexes. Avoid when they only ask to list slow queries; use get_top_queries.

max_index_count.description: Optional maximum number of indexes to recommend.

tools.name: analyze_workload_indexes
tools.purpose: Recommend indexes by analyzing the observed database workload as a whole.
quality_checks: Do not use for one supplied query unless the query first came from workload analysis.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks for index recommendations across the live workload or asks to speed up the database without naming one SQL query.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for whole-workload tuning when no SQL is supplied; use analyze_workload_indexes. Avoid for plan-only questions; use explain_query.

queries.description: Specific SQL queries to tune, up to 10.

tools.name: analyze_query_indexes
tools.purpose: Recommend optimal indexes for one or more specific supplied SQL queries.
quality_checks: Use when query text is available and the user wants indexing/tuning advice.
tools.use_when: Use when the user says optimize this query, suggest indexes for this SQL, or tune these provided statements.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for one SQL query, schema inventory, object details, or top-query ranking.
tools.name: analyze_db_health
tools.purpose: Run comprehensive deterministic Postgres health checks.
quality_checks: Covers buffer cache, connections, constraints, index health, sequences, replication, and vacuum.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks for a health check, operational risk scan, or database maintenance review.

cases.name: discover schemas
cases.task: I just connected this Postgres MCP server. What schemas are available?

cases.name: list schema objects
cases.task: Show me the tables, views, sequences, and extensions inside the analytics schema.

cases.name: inspect table structure
cases.task: Before writing SQL, inspect public.orders so we know its columns, constraints, and indexes.

cases.name: run ready read query
cases.task: Run this ready query now: SELECT count(*) FROM public.orders WHERE status = 'failed';

cases.name: get query plan
cases.task: Do not run the query for rows. Show the PostgreSQL execution plan and planner cost for SELECT * FROM orders WHERE customer_id = 42;

cases.name: rank slow workload queries
cases.task: Rank the top 5 slow workload queries by total execution time.

cases.name: workload index tuning
cases.task: Analyze workload indexes with at most 3 candidate indexes.

cases.name: specific query index tuning
cases.task: Suggest the best indexes for this specific SQL: SELECT * FROM orders JOIN customers ON orders.customer_id = customers.id WHERE orders.created_at > '2026-01-01';

cases.name: database health check
cases.task: Run a database-wide operational health review covering connections, vacuum, invalid constraints, duplicate or unused indexes, sequences, and buffer cache.
